user_query:
I'm planning a four-week content plan for my research-oriented engineering blog and our lab channels, with a tone that appeals to academics and nods to the giants of science and computing I admire. Please work within the provided workspace files and produce the following deliverables under outputs/:

1) outputs/content_plan.csv — Build an 8-row plan covering W1–W4 with two slots per week taken from config/cadence.yaml:weekly_slots. Use these columns exactly: week (W1|W2|W3|W4), slot, topic_title, topic_type (repurpose|new), source_post (path if repurpose, else empty), primary_tag, primary_audience (from config/audience.yaml), distribution_channels (semicolon-separated subset of SUPPORTED_CHANNELS including "Blog"), rationale (<=200 chars). Requirements:
   - Inspect and honor constraints from scripts/site_config.py (SUPPORTED_CHANNELS, CHANNEL_LIMITS, PRIMARY_TAGS) and config/cadence.yaml (weekly_slots, rules) and map audiences from config/audience.yaml.
   - At least 2 rows must be repurposed from existing content in content/posts/; for each repurposed row, source_post must be a relative path to the chosen file and primary_tag must be one of that file’s tags.
   - Do not reuse the same primary_tag anywhere across W1–W4 (8 rows total).
   - For any new topic, choose a primary_tag from scripts/site_config.py:PRIMARY_TAGS that is not already used and that is also listed in the chosen primary_audience’s interests (from config/audience.yaml).
   - Each row’s distribution_channels must include "Blog" and optionally other supported channels. Per-week usage (counts per channel) must not exceed CHANNEL_LIMITS from scripts/site_config.py.
   - Choose primary_audience so that its interests include the row’s primary_tag.

2) outputs/channel_schedule.yaml — A summary derived from outputs/content_plan.csv that shows, for each week W1–W4: total_items and channel_counts for each supported channel. Ensure counts are consistent with the CSV and do not exceed CHANNEL_LIMITS.

3) outputs/message_drafts/outreach_email.md — Draft a 150–250 word outreach email to a PI-level academic contact inviting them to share the month’s theme. Reference the plan (point to outputs/content_plan.csv), emphasize themes like reproducibility and performance/HPC where applicable, and include a brief, respectful nod to the scientific greats that inspire this series. Keep it professional, evidence-focused, and free of real names.

4) outputs/message_drafts/lab_slack_announcement.txt — Draft a 3–5 sentence Slack announcement to lab colleagues summarizing the upcoming month’s plan, asking for feedback or contributions, and referencing outputs/content_plan.csv.

Essential steps you must perform:
- Inspect code/config files: read scripts/site_config.py for SUPPORTED_CHANNELS, CHANNEL_LIMITS, PRIMARY_TAGS; read config/cadence.yaml for weekly_slots and rules; read config/audience.yaml for audiences and their interests.
- Inspect files and directories: scan content/posts/ to inventory published posts and their tags for repurposing and to avoid duplicate primary_tag usage.
- Draft emails/messages: produce the two message drafts aligned to the plan and audience.

Notes:
- Treat weeks abstractly as W1–W4 (no calendar dates).
- Keep all outputs self-contained and based only on the provided inputs. Do not fetch external content.
- Ensure all constraints above are satisfied so that counts and selections can be cross-validated against the input files. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
